title: actlint honesty-grade policy
description: The published, reviewable policy that reduces a server's honesty findings to a single letter grade (A best, F worst). Pure data: the grade is computed by the reporters from this table, never hand-tuned, so 'why is this a C?' is always answerable from the policy. The grade measures labelling HONESTY, not safety. A band change that reclassifies servers is a MAJOR version event.

version.description: Semver of the grade policy — an independent line, versioned alongside the severity policy and crosswalk.

note.description: A plain-language reminder that this grades honesty, not safety, so a reviewer reads the numbers in the right frame.

weights.description: The honesty weight of one tool, keyed by its worst per-tool verdict. The server score is the weighted mean over all tools. The ordering IS the product's asymmetry as data: a consistent tool is fully honest (1.0); an under-declared tool actively misleads and weighs least. undeclared (silent) and over-declared (honest but noisy) sit between.

bands.description: Score-to-letter bands, listed best grade first. A grade is the first band whose minScore the server's honesty score meets or exceeds. The lowest band must have minScore 0 so every score lands somewhere.

caps.description: Ceilings the worst dishonesty imposes regardless of score. A server with any actively-dishonest (under-declared) tool cannot be graded better than anyUnderDeclared; one whose under-declaration removed a spec-default safety prompt (critical severity) cannot beat criticalUnderDeclared. The strictest applicable cap wins.
